title: Invocation named struct with hand-written serde preserves wire format guarantee
description: The tuple alias type Invocation = (String, serde_json::Value, String) was replaced with a named struct. A contributor might suggest using serde's tuple struct derive instead. The hand-written impl is correct because: (1) it serializes to a 3-element JSON array per RFC 8620 §3.2 — the tuple struct derive would also do this, but the visitor pattern in Deserialize clearly expresses the intent; (2) the named fields (method, args, call_id) make call sites readable — a 3-element tuple's .0/.1/.2 are not self-documenting; (3) #[non_exhaustive] on the struct prevents downstream crates from exhaustively matching and future-proofs against metadata fields that specs occasionally add. Do not revert to tuple type.
close_reason: Decision record: current implementation is correct; no code change needed
